adds.rationale: Opens the watchlist's first quantum-computing position; IonQ is the most commercially advanced US-listed pure-play and is in clear buildup phase per the user's wave thesis.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ reports Q1 2025 revenue of $7.6M above the midpoint of guidance, ends quarter with $697M cash, reaffirms full-year 2025 revenue guide of $75-95M.
IonQ closes acquisition of Lightsynq and announces proposed acquisition of Oxford Ionics, building an integrated quantum computing and networking roadmap.
IonQ achieves world-record 99.99% two-qubit gate fidelity, a concrete technical milestone toward fault-tolerant systems.

removes.rationale: Thematic redundancy with BOTZ: ARK Autonomous Tech & Robotics overlaps heavily with BOTZ on robotics/automation exposure, and BOTZ's index-based construction (Nvidia, Keyence, ABB, Fanuc, Intuitive Surgical) covers the wave more cleanly with lower active-management drag, freeing a slot for quantum.

news_evidence.summary: ARKQ's Q1 2025 holdings concentrate in autonomous/robotics names (Kratos, AeroVironment, Tesla) that overlap BOTZ's industrial-automation core, with limited diversification benefit at the watchlist level.
ARKQ's H1 2025 returns lagged its broader robotics-ETF peer set as active stock-picks like Tesla weighed on performance versus index-based BOTZ.

rationale_overall: Swap ARKQ (redundant with BOTZ in the robotics/automation bucket) for IONQ (citable Q1 2025 commercial milestones, $697M balance sheet, two pre-date M&A actions, and a world-record fidelity result).
